paper_title: Coupled Mass Balance and Ice Flow Model for Glacier Dynamics

methods.method_name: Coupled Mass Balance and Ice Flow Model Simulation
methods.description: This method simulates glacier dynamics and thermodynamic processes using a digital elevation model (DEM) of the bedrock. It aims to reconstruct paleoclimate conditions by adjusting temperature and precipitation parameters to match historical glacial extents.

steps.description: A DEM for the study area with 30 × 30 m spatial resolution was downloaded from the Geospatial Data Cloud and trimmed to the watersheds of the model domain.
steps.searchable_summary: download and trim DEM data from Geospatial Data Cloud

steps.description: Altitudinal gradients of monthly averaged temperature and precipitation were obtained from the global climate dataset created by Fick and Hijmans (2017).
steps.searchable_summary: fetch altitudinal climate data from global dataset

steps.description: The mass balance model simulates the glacier mass balance in the model domain with these altitudinal gradients of monthly averaged temperature and precipitation.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate glacier mass balance using climate data

steps.description: To reconstruct the paleoclimate conditions, the difference in the mean monthly air temperature from the present-day conditions (ΔT) and fractional value of the present-day precipitation (F p ) were adjusted to match the positions of t1.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust temperature and precipitation to match historical glacial extents

steps.description: Different DDF values (6-9 mm d −1°C−1 ) were used to test the model sensitivity to changes in DDF.
steps.searchable_summary: test model sensitivity with varying DDF values

methods.description: This method evaluates the sensitivity of the coupled mass balance and ice flow model to changes in key parameters and assesses the uncertainties in paleoclimate reconstructions.

steps.description: An experiment was conducted to test the model sensitivity to changes in DDF based on simulations of t1 in V2.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct sensitivity test for DDF changes

steps.description: Variations of ± 1°C in T s should yield changes of <.1°C on the model results.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model sensitivity to T s variations

steps.description: Errors in the details of the ice motion physics have only a minor effect on our modeled paleoclimate estimates due to the assumption of a steady-state paleoglacier.
steps.searchable_summary: assess impact of ice motion physics errors

steps.description: Uncertainties in the model input data sets produce errors for ΔT and F p of ± .5°C and ± .3, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate uncertainties in model input data
